title: Userdomain Variable Enumeration
falsepositives: Certain scripts or applications may leverage this.

title: Password Protected Compressed File Extraction Via 7Zip
falsepositives: Legitimate activity is expected since extracting files with a password can be common in some environment.

title: Set Files as System Files Using Attrib.EXE
CMD Shell Output Redirect
falsepositives: Internet Download Manager extensions use named pipes and redirection via CLI. Filter it out if you use it in your environment

title: Potential File Override/Append Via SET Command
falsepositives: Legitimate use of the SET with the "/p" flag for user prompting. command in administrative scripts or user-generated scripts.

title: Dynamic .NET Compilation Via Csc.EXE - Hunting
falsepositives: Many legitimate applications make use of dynamic compilation. Use this rule to hunt for anomalies

title: File Download Via Curl.EXE
falsepositives: Scripts created by developers and admins
The "\Git\usr\bin\sh.exe" process uses the "--output" flag to download a specific file in the temp directory with the pattern "gfw-httpget-xxxxxxxx.txt "

title: Potential Data Exfiltration Via Curl.EXE
falsepositives: Scripts created by developers and admins

title: Curl.EXE Execution With Custom UserAgent
falsepositives: Scripts created by developers and admins

title: Potential Proxy Execution Via Explorer.EXE From Shell Process
falsepositives: Legitimate explorer.exe run from a shell host like "cmd.exe" or "powershell.exe"

title: New Self Extracting Package Created Via IExpress.EXE
Invocation Of Crypto-Classes From The "Cryptography" PowerShell Namespace
falsepositives: Classes are legitimately used, but less so when e.g. parents with low prevalence or decryption of content in temporary folders.

title: Import New Module Via PowerShell CommandLine
falsepositives: Depending on the environement, many legitimate scripts will import modules inline. This rule is targeted for hunting purposes.

title: New Windows Firewall Rule Added Via New-NetFirewallRule Cmdlet
Regsvr32.EXE Calling of DllRegisterServer Export Function Implicitly
falsepositives: Legitimate usage as part of application installation, but less likely from e.g. temporary paths.

title: Remote Access Tool - Ammy Admin Agent Execution
falsepositives: Legitimate use of Ammy Admin RMM agent for remote management by admins.

title: DLL Call by Ordinal Via Rundll32.EXE
falsepositives: False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment.
Windows control panel elements have been identified as source (mmc).

title: Rundll32.EXE Calling DllRegisterServer Export Function Explicitly
falsepositives: Legitimate usage as part of application installation, but less likely from e.g. temporary paths.
Not every instance is considered malicious, but this rule will capture the malicious usages.

title: SC.EXE Query Execution
falsepositives: Legitimate query of a service by an administrator to get more information such as the state or PID
Keybase process "kbfsdokan.exe" query the dokan1 service with the following commandline "sc query dokan1"

title: Scheduled Task Creation From Potential Suspicious Parent Location
falsepositives: Software installers that run from temporary folders and also install scheduled tasks

title: Potential CommandLine Obfuscation Using Unicode Characters
EventLog Query Requests By Builtin Utilities
falsepositives: Legitimate log access by administrators or troubleshooting tools

title: Potential Suspicious Execution From GUID Like Folder Names
falsepositives: Installers are sometimes known for creating temporary folders with GUID like names. Add appropriate filters accordingly

title: File or Folder Permissions Modifications
falsepositives: Users interacting with the files on their own (unlikely unless privileged users).

title: Use Short Name Path in Command Line
falsepositives: Applications could use this notation occasionally which might generate some false positives. In that case investigate the parent and child process.

title: HTML File Opened From Download Folder
falsepositives: Opening any HTML file located in users directories via a browser process will trigger this.

title: Manual Execution of Script Inside of a Compressed File
falsepositives: Batch files may produce a lot of noise, as many applications appear to bundle them as part of their installation process. You should baseline your environment and generate a new query excluding the noisy and expected activity. Some false positives may come up depending on your environment. All results should be investigated thoroughly before filtering out results.

title: Process Terminated Via Taskkill
falsepositives: Expected FP with some processes using this techniques to terminate one of their processes during installations and updates

title: Suspicious Tasklist Discovery Command
falsepositives: Likely from users, administrator and different internal and third party applications.

title: FTP Connection Open Attempt Via Winscp CLI
WSF/JSE/JS/VBA/VBE File Execution Via Cscript/Wscript
falsepositives: Some additional tuning is required. It is recommended to add the user profile path in CommandLine if it is getting too noisy.

title: Arbitrary Command Execution Using WSL
falsepositives: Automation and orchestration scripts may use this method to execute scripts etc.
Legitimate use by Windows to kill processes opened via WSL (example VsCode WSL server)

title: Cab File Extraction Via Wusa.EXE
falsepositives: The "extract" flag still works on older 'wusa.exe' versions, which could be a legitimate use (monitor the path of the cab being extracted)

title: Audit Rules Deleted Via Auditctl
falsepositives: An administrator troubleshooting. Investigate all attempts.

falsepositives: System administrator manually stopping Kaspersky services

falsepositives: Legitimate usage of the unsafe option
